commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Enable HTTP server observations and histogram distribution so that

on the actuator/prometheus endpoint.

Fix post creation to use JSON request body instead of query params

Add @RequestBody to FeedController, send content as JSON body in load
test client, and configure Redis ObjectMapper with JavaTimeModule for
proper date serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement news feed system with fanout architecture

Spring Boot 3.4 / Java 21 app with Nginx load balancer, Post Service
(PostgreSQL + Redis cache), Fanout Service (RabbitMQ → workers → News Feed
Cache), Neo4j graph DB for friendships, Prometheus/Grafana monitoring,
load test client, unit tests, and Docker Compose orchestration.

regardless of whether it's auto-scroll or manual user scrolling.

commit.message:
Phases 4–7: 3D Turtle, Species Catalog, Geometry, glTF Export (#18)

Build the complete 3D pipeline from L-string to exportable mesh:

Phase 4 — 3D Turtle Interpreter (ABOP §1.5.3):
- LineSegment3D, TurtleState3D with HLU orthonormal frame
- interpret3d() with yaw/pitch/roll/turn-around commands
- Rodrigues' rotation + periodic Gram-Schmidt re-orthonormalization

Phase 5 — Species Catalog:
- LSystemDef struct with full species metadata
- Global registry with register_species!, get_species, list_species
- 100 L-systems extracted from gallery script into categorized files
- Gallery script refactored to consume from catalog

Phase 6 — Geometry Module:
- TriangleMesh struct, cylinder_mesh() (truncated cones)
- merge_meshes(), segments_to_mesh() pipeline
- BoundingBox3D with compute_bbox for 3D segments

Phase 7 — glTF Export:
- export_glb() for binary glTF 2.0 output
- render_lsystem_3d() end-to-end pipeline
- JSON3 dependency added

388 new tests (82 turtle3d + 136 geometry + 22 gltf + 148 species).

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: use direct hex values in @theme inline for purple utilities

Tailwind v4 requires direct color values (not var() references to hex CSS
variables) in @theme inline to reliably generate bg/text/border utilities.

Reframe entire site around governance as the killer feature

Polyglot is the mechanism, not the selling point. No other language
has built-in AI governance at the runtime level. Hero, features,
process flow, and competitive comparison all rewritten to lead with
governance-first messaging. Competitive positioning against linters,
SAST, Guardrails AI, prompt engineering, and code review.

commit.message:
chore: version intermediate build artifacts and store uncompressed (#25)

* chore: version intermediate build artifacts and store uncompressed

Read FW_VERSION_MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH from version.h at build time,
rename the binary to esp32-weather-leds-v{VERSION}-dev.bin before
uploading, and use that as the artifact name. Sets compression-level: 0
so the binary is stored rather than additionally compressed.

Closes #24

* chore: include build timestamp in intermediate artifact name

Artifact and binary are now named
esp32-weather-leds-v{VERSION}-dev-{YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS}.bin
so builds are easy to identify when downloading for a quick test.
Removed compression-level override; default compression is fine.

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: add report/flag buttons with DMCA categories + fix opponent team manual entry

- Add report button to MasonryPostCard and post-detail with copyright/DMCA report categories
- Update PostCard report reasons to include copyright infringement, broadcast footage, unauthorized likeness
- Fix opponent team picker: manual entry option now always visible when searching (not just on empty results)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: model/dossier shedding archaeology + daimon summoning

Two features extending Claudicle's memory and daimonic layers:

1. Model Shedding: When user models or dossiers are updated, the prior
   version is preserved with structured diffs, internal monologue context,
   and optional LLM-generated meta commentary. Creates an archaeological
   record of identity evolution in a model_sheds SQLite table.

2. Daimon Summoning: Any entity (user model, person dossier, subject
   dossier) can be summoned as an ephemeral speaking daimon. Soul.md is
   synthesized from entity content using type-specific templates, cached
   in memory (no filesystem writes), and registered in the daimon registry
   for Groq whisper invocation. Three interfaces: autonomous cognitive
   step, /daimon slash command, and programmatic API.

New files: model_journal.py, summoning.py, test_model_journal.py,
test_summoning.py, agent_docs/model-archaeology-summoning.md.

Modified: config.py (6 new settings), steps.py (3 new cognitive steps),
soul_engine.py (summon extraction + assembly), snapshot.py (direct summon
execution in apply_output), user_models.py (shed hooks), reflect.py
(context threading), working_memory.py (modelShed rendering),
daimon.md (summon/dismiss/summoned subcommands).

923 tests passing (53 new).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace div onClick with stretched Link for crawlable card navigation

Google couldn't follow the div+onClick pattern. Now each card has a real
<a href> (via Next.js Link) covering the full card area, so crawlers see
and follow the link. Share/flag buttons sit above it with z-10 so they
still intercept clicks correctly.

commit.message:
feat: Level 1 curve fitting pipeline with RDP simplification + Chaikin smoothing

Add a streaming fidelity pipeline between raw stylus samples (Level 0, 240Hz)
and the raster cache (Level 2). On pen-up, raw points are simplified via
multi-dimensional Ramer-Douglas-Peucker (preserving pressure/tilt events),
then smoothed with Chaikin corner-cutting subdivision to eliminate faceting
on small/quick circles.

- New CurveFitter utility with RDP simplify() and chaikinSmooth()
- Stroke model: fittedPoints field + renderPoints getter with serialization
- Chapter model: archiveRawData flag for future cloud sync control
- Database v3 migration: fitted_points_blob + archive_raw_data columns
- Renderer uses stroke.renderPoints; active stroke still uses raw points
- 27 new tests covering simplification, smoothing, and serialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add ntm-unstall skill

Recovers stalled beads abandoned by dead agents — finds in-progress
beads with no recent activity, resets them to open, notifies the swarm,
then uses bv robot flags to pick up the highest-impact work immediately.

Addresses the velocity bottleneck where zombie in-progress beads block
the queue and agents spin on empty ready lists.

fix: parse Ring JSON:API compound document correctly for device discovery

The Ring API returns device status in the `included` array (not in
device.attributes) per JSON:API spec. Updated fetchRingDevices to build
an included-resource lookup map and resolve status/capabilities from it.

Also added better error logging for device discovery during linking,
and a POST /api/ring/devices endpoint to re-sync devices on demand.

* refactor(relationships): redesign progression to temporary/permanent/capacity model

Replace single-points system with three-tier progression:
- Relationship Updates (unlimited): add temporary points + capacity, decay 10%/day
- Development Updates (7/week): add permanent points up to capacity, award XP
- Capstone Events (unlimited): add permanent points + capacity simultaneously

New models: RelationshipDevelopment, RelationshipCapstone
Updated: RelationshipTrackProgress (capacity + developed_points), CharacterRelationship
(developments_this_week), RelationshipUpdate (current_temporary_value decay method)
Mechanical bonus: cube root of developed absolute value

commit.message:
feat: Photo Studio v2 — data-driven recipe engine with 28 styles

Replace hardcoded Python style functions with a JSON recipe system
(styles.json). Adding a new style = adding a JSON entry, zero code changes.

28 styles across 4 categories: Film Stocks (Portra, Ektar, Tri-X,
CineStill, Pro 400H, etc.), Cinematic (Wong Kar-wai, Blade Runner,
Wes Anderson, Ghibli, etc.), Photographers (Moriyama, Fan Ho, Saul
Leiter, Vivian Maier), and 2026 Trending (Moody Natural, Soft Film,
Clean & Bright, Faded Editorial, etc.).

New capabilities:
- Generic apply_recipe() engine interprets JSON recipes
- Style preview generation (400px thumbnails for all styles)
- Single-photo processing via enso_media_process_single_photo
- apply_style now performs real pixel processing (was metadata-only)
- Enhanced photo book with aspect-ratio-aware layouts (hero, grid,
  editorial, panoramic, minimal page types)
- Tabbed style picker in template (Film Stocks | Cinematic |
  Photographers | Trending)
- New recipe features: halation, split tone, luminance-aware grain,
  shadow color push, double S-curve, green-to-teal, haze highlights

- Remove incorrect "5-8 tools recommended" MCP spec citation
- Add "Go owns data / Claude owns interpretation" invariant
- Fix all function signatures: []parser.SessionEntry (not []types.Entry)
- Add loadEntriesAndToolCalls() helper pattern for Phase 42-44
- Add CleanupSessionCache() relocation to Stage 45.1 (atomic with executor cleanup)
- Fix Stage 45.1 call sites: capability tools are in executor.go:81,91
- Fix TDD semantics: "expect BUILD FAIL" for undefined functions in Go
- Fix Stage 46.1/46.2 ordering: update manifests first, delete files second
- Add capabilities_cache_test.go (53 lines) to removal table; total ~10,019 lines
- Add parallel execution caveat for Phases 42-44

refine order detail modal: 2-col info grid for IDs and dates

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Approval Inbox — /approvals GET + /approval-respond POST + dashboard banners

- GET /approvals: shows pending gates for the logged-in user (any role),
  with inline approve/reject form, exam/sitting/gate/submitter info, View Exam link,
  and a read-only Recent section for already-responded items
- POST /approval-respond: validates gate assignment, upserts the response row
  (status, note, updated_at), redirects to /approvals
- [[path]].js: registers /approvals and /approval-respond in the sittings block
- app.js: adds a pending-count banner (📬 You have X pending approvals) at the
  top of /school, /teacher, and /student dashboards; banner hidden when count = 0
- All queries are tenant-isolated; no new DB tables

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
